rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) + Cowork.

rules_evaluated.note: Line 33: 'Claude can wait for other group files and re-read as new comments arrive' — a Cowork polling/loop capability claim. reference_cowork_capabilities.md marks /loop UNVERIFIED, but the sourced exercise (find-the-crux.md line 190) carries an explicit maintainer-acceptance for this loop shape ('Cowork supports this loop shape per Antti's direct knowledge'). The module body summarises exercise behaviour; the verification lives upstream in the exercise's maintainer block. Treated as PASS under §5 (practitioner observation beats docs lookup).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cowork is sandboxed to its connected working directories.
rules_evaluated.note: File reads and writes stay inside the connected SharePoint folder. No out-of-sandbox reads claimed.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pseudo-URI + kill-switch for public/private content split.
rules_evaluated.note: Line 58 maintainer block: 'bosser-strategy:content-strategy-claude-basics.md' — correct pseudo-URI format, not a filesystem path.

Line 30: 'Richard Rumelt calls this the crux' — no URL, no source-type label. Per §1 cultural-vocab carve-out, Rumelt's 'crux' concept qualifies as cultural vocabulary (public-domain strategy thinker, idea woven into practitioner vocabulary independent of a single artifact). Source-type labels are also maintainer-block vocabulary, not student-facing body vocabulary (per §1 scope rule). This is clean.
No fenced prompt blocks and no {{prompt:...}} markers in this file. Surface 4 (prompt design mechanics) does not apply.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Prompts live in the registry; module files reference them by marker.
rules_evaluated.note: File contains no inline prompt blocks and no prompt registry markers. Consistent with claude-basics training convention (inlined prompts live in exercise files, not module wrappers).
Line 26: '[Exercise: Find the rollout challenge](exercises/find-the-crux.md)' — standalone paragraph include-link. File confirmed at curriculum/exercises/find-the-crux.md.

claude-basics is chat-level audience (IT rollout custodians). No skills, no install affordances, no plugin references in this module. Consistent with training-level substrate decision (plain .md prompt files, inlined in exercise).

platform_claims_checked.claim: Claude can wait for other group files and re-read as new comments arrive
platform_claims_checked.note: Cowork loop capability. /loop is UNVERIFIED in reference_cowork_capabilities.md but find-the-crux.md line 190 carries explicit maintainer-acceptance for this behaviour per Antti's direct knowledge. Module body summarises exercise behaviour; upstream verification is the governing source. §5 practitioner-observation applies.
Correct pseudo-URI format per check_platform_and_boundaries.md §13. Not a filesystem path leak.

citations_checked.claim: Richard Rumelt calls this the crux: the one thing that, if you get it right, makes the rest follow
citations_checked.note: Cultural-vocabulary carve-out applies (check_research_claims.md §1). Rumelt's 'crux' is community vocabulary independent of a single artifact; [cultural-vocab] closes the tracker without a URL. Source-type labels are maintainer-block vocabulary, not student-facing body. Clean.

todos.issue: Eval instance path 'curriculum/evals/instances/claude-basics--find-the-crux.md' references .md extension but actual eval instances for this training use .json (e.g. claude-basics--find-the-crux.pedagogy.json). The training CLAUDE.md also states the .md convention, so this is a convention-level staleness, not a module-file error. Non-blocking: reference is in maintainer block only, no student-facing impact. Resolve when the training CLAUDE.md convention is updated to reflect .json.
